original_hypothesis.title: Collection namespace overlap possible at boundary totalSupply==1e10, enabling tokenId collisions across collections
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: NextGenCore.setCollectionData allows _collectionTotalSupply up to 10,000,000,000 and sets reservedMinTokensIndex = collectionID*1e10 and reservedMaxTokensIndex = collectionID*1e10 + totalSupply - 1. If totalSupply equals 1e10, reservedMaxTokensIndex becomes (collectionID+1)*1e10 - 1, which abuts/overlaps the next collection’s namespace boundary assumptions and can create collisions if other collections also use boundary values or if indexing logic assumes strict separation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if not used in typical configs, this is a clear correctness defect at a boundary value explicitly permitted by require(_collectionTotalSupply <= 10000000000). TokenId collisions are catastrophic for ERC721 uniqueness and collection attribution.
deployment_assumptions: An admin sets _collectionTotalSupply to 10,000,000,000 for at least one collection
At least one subsequent collection is created and configured normally using the same 1e10 stride
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Code explicitly permits <= 10,000,000,000 in setCollectionData; no extra guard enforcing < 1e10.

proof_of_signal.pattern: allow totalSupply <= 1e10; reservedMin = colId*1e10; reservedMax = colId*1e10 + totalSupply - 1

preconditions: Admin configures a collection with totalSupply == 10,000,000,000
Another collection exists such that tokenId ranges become adjacent/colliding in downstream logic

initial_code_evidence.suspicious_location: NextGenCore.sol setCollectionData token range math at boundary
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: token IDs could collide or violate 'non-overlap' assumption used in metadata and supply logic
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Require totalSupply < 1e10 (strict) or use larger stride

analysis_questions: Does any other logic assume strict non-overlap (e.g., viewColIDforTokenID mapping correctness, reserved ranges used for burn validation)?
Can collisions occur in practice given how tokenIdsToCollectionIds is set at mint time (mapping would store last writer)?
Would ERC721 _safeMint revert on existing tokenId, turning this into a DoS rather than a collision?
validation_requirements: Simulate two collections with boundary supplies and attempt to mint tokenIds at the edges
Observe whether _safeMint reverts due to existing tokenId, and whether this can brick later collections

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If misconfigured to allow 1e10 supply, later collections’ intended min token IDs may already be within an earlier collection’s max range, causing mint failures or misattribution at the boundary.
prerequisites: Admin misconfiguration (or intentional) at extreme boundary

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Medium: could DoS minting for later collections or break collection attribution at edges
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Minters and collectors of affected collections

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No boundary tests for tokenId namespace separation at totalSupply==1e10

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Check configured supplies and reserved ranges for overlap

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
